for YAML processing.\n2\t//\n3\t// Error Hierarchy:\n4\t//\n5\t//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n6\t//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n7\t//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n8\t//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n9\t//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n10\t//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n11\t//\t├── ValidationError (validation (\n21\t\t\"errors\"\n22\t\t\"fmt\"\n23\t\t\"os\"\n24\t\t\"strings\"\n25\t)\n26\t\n27\t// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n28\t//\n29\t// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n30\t// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\n31\ttype YAMLError interface {\n32\t\terror\n33\t\n34\t\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n35\t\tCode() ErrorCode\n36\t\n37\t\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n38\t\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n39\t\n40\t\t// Context (\n48\t\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n49\t\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n50\t\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n51\t\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n52\t\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n53\t\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n54\t\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n55\t\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n56\t\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n57\t\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n58\t\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n59\t\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n60\t\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n61\t\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n62\t\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n63\t)\n64\t\n65\t// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n66\t//\n67\t// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n68\t// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n84\t\t//\n85\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n86\t\t// expected Go type, including:\n87\t\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n88\t\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n89\t\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n90\t\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n91\t\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n92\t\n93\t\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n94\t\t//\n95\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n96\t\t// including:\n97\t\t// - Value out of allowed range\n98\t\t// - String length violations\n99\t\t// - Pattern matching failures\n100\t\t// - Required field violations\n101\t\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n102\t\n103\t\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n104\t\t//\n105\t\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n106\t\t// including:\n107\t\t// - File not found\n108\t\t// - Permission denied\n109\t\t// - Read/write failures\n110\t\t// - File system errors\n111\t\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n112\t\n113\t\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n114\t\t//\n115\t\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n116\t\t// including:\n117\t\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n118\t\t// - Invalid nesting\n119\t\t// - Circular references\n120\t\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n121\t\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n122\t\n123\t\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n124\t\t//\n125\t\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n126\t\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n127\t\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n128\t\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n129\t\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n130\t\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n131\t)\n132\t\n133\t// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\n134\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n135\t\treturn string(v)\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\n139\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n140\t\tswitch v {\n141\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n142\t\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// ============================================================================\n166\t\t// File Error Codes\n167\t\t// ============================================================================\n168\t\n169\t\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n170\t\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n171\t\t// be found in the filesystem.\n172\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n173\t\n174\t\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n175\t\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n176\t\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n177\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n178\t\n179\t\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n180\t\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n181\t\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n182\t\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n183\t\n184\t\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n185\t\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n186\t\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n187\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n188\t\n189\t\t// ============================================================================\n190\t\t// Parse Error Codes\n191\t\t// ============================================================================\n192\t\n193\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n194\t\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n195\t\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n196\t\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n197\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n198\t\n199\t\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n200\t\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n201\t\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n202\t\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n203\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n204\t\n205\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n206\t\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n207\t\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n208\t\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n209\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n210\t\n211\t\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n212\t\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n213\t\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n214\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n215\t\n216\t\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n217\t\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n218\t\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n219\t\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n220\t\n221\t\t// ============================================================================\n222\t\t// Validation Error Codes\n223\t\t// ============================================================================\n224\t\n225\t\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n226\t\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n227\t\t// validation error categories.\n228\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n229\t\n230\t\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n231\t\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n232\t\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n233\t\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n234\t\n235\t\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n236\t\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n237\t\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n238\t\t// schema or validation logic.\n239\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n240\t\n241\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n242\t\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n243\t\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n244\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n245\t\n246\t\t// ============================================================================\n247\t\t// Schema Error Codes\n248\t\t// ============================================================================\n249\t\n250\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n251\t\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n252\t\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n253\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n254\t\n255\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n256\t\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n257\t\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n258\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n259\t\n260\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n261\t\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n262\t\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n263\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n264\t\n265\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n266\t\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n267\t\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n268\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n269\t)\n270\t\n271\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n272\t//\n273\t// ParseError wraps (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n343\t\treturn pe.Err\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n347\t//\n348\t// This constructor function provides a empty string if not applicable)\n358\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n359\t//\n360\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n361\t//\n362\t// Example usage:\n363\t//\n364\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n365\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n366\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n367\t\terrorCode := code\n368\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n369\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\treturn &ParseError{\n373\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n374\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n375\t\t\tLine:      line,\n376\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n377\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n378\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n379\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n380\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n381\t\t}\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n385\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n386\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n387\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n391\t//\n392\t// ValidationError wraps        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n405\t\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n406\t\tType         ErrorType // Category of error {\n421\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n422\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n423\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n424\t\t}\n425\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n426\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n427\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n428\t\t}\n429\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation chains.\n471\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n472\t\treturn ve.Err\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n476\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n477\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n478\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n479\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n480\t\tif %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n495\t\t}\n496\t\treturn sb.String()\n497\t}\n498\t\n499\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n500\t//\n501\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n502\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n503\t//\n504\t// Parameters:\n505\t// Constraint that was violated (optional)\n509\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n510\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n511\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n512\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n513\t//   - path: Dot-notation field path (optional, for backward compatibility defaults to empty string)\n514\t//\n515\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n516\t//\n517\t// Example usage:\n518\t//\n519\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\", \"spec.replicas\")\n520\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType, path string) *ValidationError {\n521\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n522\t\terrorCode := code\n523\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n524\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n525\t\t}\n526\t\n527\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n528\t\teType := errorType\n529\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n530\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n534\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n535\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n536\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n537\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n538\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n539\t\t\tLine:       line,\n540\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n541\t\t\tType:       eType,\n542\t\t\tPath:       path,\n543\t\t}\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n547\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n548\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n549\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n553\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n554\t\treturn &FileError{\n555\t\t\tPath:       path,\n556\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n557\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n558\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n559\t\t}\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\n563\tfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n564\t\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n565\t\t\tFilePath: line,\n572\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n573\t\t}\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\n577\tfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath    errorCode,\n586\t\t}\n587\t}\n588\t\n589\t// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\n590\tfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, errorCode,\n596\t\t}\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\n600\tfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, value,\n608\t\t\tLine:           line,\n609\t\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n610\t\t}\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper initialization.\n614\tfunc with proper initialization.\n639\t//\n640\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n641\t// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n642\t//\n643\t// Parameters:\n644\t//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n645\t//   - key: The duplicate key name\n646\t//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key &SchemaLoadError{\n689\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n690\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n691\t\t\tErr:       err,\n692\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n693\t\t}\n694\t}\n695\t\n696\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n697\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n698\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n699\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n700\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n701\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n702\t\t\t\treturn true\n703\t\t\t}\n704\t\t}\n705\t\treturn false\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n709\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n710\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n711\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n712\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n713\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n714\t\t\t\treturn true\n715\t\t\t}\n716\t\t}\n717\t\treturn false\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n721\t//\n722\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n723\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n724\ttype FileError struct {\n725\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n726\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n727\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n728\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n729\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n730\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n734\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n735\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n736\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n737\t\t}\n738\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n739\t}\n740\t\n741\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n742\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n743\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n744\t}\n745\t\n746\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n747\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n748\t\top := fe.Operation\n749\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n750\t\t\top = fe.Op\n751\t\t}\n752\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n753\t}\n754\t\n755\t// Error implements the error interface.\n756\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n757\t\top := fe.Operation\n758\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n759\t\t\top = fe.Op\n760\t\t}\n761\t\n762\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n763\t\tif op != \"\" {\n764\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n765\t\t} else {\n766\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n767\t\t}\n768\t\n769\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n770\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n771\t\t}\n772\t\n773\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n774\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\treturn sb.String()\n778\t}\n779\t\n780\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n781\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n782\t\treturn fe.Err\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n786\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n787\t\tvar fe *FileError\n788\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n792\t//\n793\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n794\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n795\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n796\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n797\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n798\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n799\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n800\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n801\t\tFound Code implements YAMLError interface.\n807\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n808\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n809\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n810\t\t}\n811\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n817\t}\n818\t\n819\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n820\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n821\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n822\t\tif fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n834\t}\n835\t\n836\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n837\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n838\t\treturn configuration error.\n1037\ttype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n1038\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n1039\t\tKey       string    // The duplicate key *DuplicateKeyError) Context() string {\n1061\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key: %s\", dke.Key)\n1062\t\tif dke.Location != \"\" {\n1063\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", tt.expected)\n304\t\t\t\t}\n305\t\t\t\tif err.Actual != tt.actual {\n306\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Actual = %q, want %q\", err.Actual, tt.actual)\n307\t\t\t\t}\n308\t\t\t\tif err.Code() != tt.wantCode {\n309\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Code() = %q, want %q\", err.Code(), tt.wantCode)\n310\t\t\t\t}\n311\t\t\t\tif err.YAMLErrorType() != tt.wantErrorType {\n312\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"YAMLErrorType() = %q, want errorMsg != tt.wantMessage {\n318\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", errorMsg, tt.wantMessage)\n319\t\t\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\t\t\t// Verify it's recognized as a YAMLError\n322\t\t\t\tif !IsParseError(err) {\n328\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"NewParseError() should be recognized as ParseError\")\n329\t\t\t\t}\n330\t\t\t})\n331\t\t}\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t// Helper function to check if a string contains a substring\n335\tfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n336\t\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || len(s) > len(substr) && containsHelper(s, substr))\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\tfunc containsHelper(s, substr string) bool {\n340\t\tfor i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {\n341\t\t\tif s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {\n342\t\t\t\treturn true\n343\t\t\t}\n344\t\t}\n345\t\treturn false\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\tfunc TestNewValidationError(t *testing.T) {\n349\t\ttests := []struct {\n350\t\t\tname         string\n351\t\t\tfilePath     string\n352\t\t\tmessage {\n409\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Context() = %q, want %q\", ctxMsg, expectedCtx)\n410\t\t}\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\t// TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes documents type mismatch errors for various type combinations\n414\t//\n415\t// Common type mismatches:\n416\t// - Expected integer, got string\n417\t// - Expected string, got integer\n418\t// - Expected boolean, got string\n419\t// - Expected array, got scalar\n420\t// - Expected object, got null\n421\tfunc TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes(t *testing.T) {\n422\t\ttestCases := []struct {\n423\t\t\tname         string\n424\t\t\tfieldPath    string\n425\t\t\texpectedType string\n426\t\t\tactualType   string\n427\t\t\tvalue        string\n428\t\t}{\n429\t\t\t{\n430\t\t\t\tname:         \"integer expected, string got\",\n431\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"port\",\n432\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"integer\",\n433\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n434\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"8080\",\n435\t\t\t},\n436\t\t\t{\n437\t\t\t\tname:         \"integer expected, boolean got\",\n438\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"timeout\",\n439\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"integer\",\n440\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"boolean\",\n441\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"true\",\n442\t\t\t},\n443\t\t\t{\n444\t\t\t\tname:         \"boolean expected, string got\",\n445\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"enabled\",\n446\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"boolean\",\n447\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n448\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"yes\",\n449\t\t\t},\n450\t\t\t{\n451\t\t\t\tname:         \"array expected, string got\",\n452\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"hosts\",\n453\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"array\",\n454\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n455\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"localhost\",\n456\t\t\t},\n457\t\t\t{\n458\t\t\t\tname:         \"object expected, null got\",\n459\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"config\",\n460\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"object\",\n461\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"null\",\n462\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"null\",\n463\t\t\t},\n464\t\t}\n465\t\n466\t\tfor _, tt := range testCases {\n467\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n468\t\t\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n469\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n470\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    tt.fieldPath,\n471\t\t\t\t\tExpectedType: tt.expectedType,\n472\t\t\t\t\tActualType:   tt.actualType,\n473\t\t\t\t\tValue:        tt.value,\n474\t\t\t\t\tLine:         10,\n475\t\t\t\t}\n476\t\n477\t\t\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n478\t\n479\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.fieldPath) {\n480\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include field path '%s'\", tt.fieldPath)\n481\t\t\t\t}\n482\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.expectedType) {\n483\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include expected type '%s'\", tt.expectedType)\n484\t\t\t\t}\n485\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.actualType) {\n486\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include actual type '%s'\", tt.actualType)\n487\t\t\t\t}\n488\t\n489\t\t\t\t// Verify error type\n490\t\t\t\tif err.YAMLErrorType() != ErrorTypeTypeMismatch {\n491\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"YAMLErrorType() = %q, want %q\", err.YAMLErrorType(), ErrorTypeTypeMismatch)\n492\t\t\t\t}\n493\t\n494\t\t\t\t// Verify error code\n495\t\t\t\tif err.Code() != ErrCodeTypeMismatch {\n496\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Code() = %q, want %q\", err.Code(), ErrCodeTypeMismatch)\n497\t\t\t\t}\n498\t\t\t})\n499\t\t}\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// TestTypeMismatchNestedFields demonstrates type mismatch in nested field paths\n503\t//\n504\t// Example:\n505\t//\n506\t//\ttype mismatch in app.yaml at line 35, field servers.api.responses[0].statusCode: expected integer, got string\n507\tfunc TestTypeMismatchNestedFields(t *testing.T) {\n508\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n509\t\t\tFilePath:     \"app.yaml\",\n510\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"servers.api.responses[0].statusCode\",\n511\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n512\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n513\t\t\tValue:        \"\\\"200\\\"\",\n514\t\t\tLine:         35,\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n518\t\n519\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"app.yaml\") {\n520\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file ============================================================================\n718\t\n719\t// TestErrorFormatConsistency verifies that error formats follow consistent patterns\n720\t//\n721\t// Consistency rules:\n722\t// 1. File path always present if available\n723\t// 2. Position (line, column) follows file path with \"at line <line>, column <column>\" format\n724\t// 3. Field paths use \"at field <path>\" or \"field <path>:\" format\n725\t// 4. Type mismatches use \"expected <expected>, got <actual>\" format\n726\t// 5. Constraints use \"(constraint: <details>)\" or \"constraint: <details>\" format\n727\tfunc TestErrorFormatConsistency(t *testing.T) {\n728\t\t// Test ParseError format consistency\n729\t\tparseErr := in'\")\n732\t\t}\n733\t\tif !contains(parseErr.Error(), \"at line 10, column 5\") {\n734\t\t\tt.Error(\"ParseError should include position with 'at line X, column Y'\")\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\t// structure\\n78\\t\\t// - Invalid escape sequences\\n79\\t\\t// - Improper quoting\\n80\\t\\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\\n81\\t\\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \\\"syntax\\\"\\n82\\t\\n83\\t\\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\\n84\\t\\t//\\n85\\t\\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\\n86\\t\\t// expected Go type, including:\\n87\\t\\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\\n88\\t\\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\\n89\\t\\t// - Incompatible pe.FilePath))\\n318\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\n320\\t\\t// Add message\\n321\\t\\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\": %s\\\", pe.Message))\\n322\\t\\n323\\t\\t// Add expected vs actual if available\\n324\\t\\tif sb.String()\\n339\\t}\\n340\\t\\n341\\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\\n342\\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\\n343\\t\\treturn pe.Err\\n344\\t}\\n345\\t\\n346\\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\\n347\\t//\\n348\\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly &ParseError{\\n373\\t\\t\\tFilePath:  filePath,\\n374\\t\\t\\tMessage:   message,\\n375\\t\\t\\tLine:      line,\\n376\\t\\t\\tColumn:    column,\\n377\\t\\t\\tErrorCode: {\\n390\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Should include file path\\\")\\n391\\t\\t}\\n392\\t\\tif !contains(errorMsg, \\\"at line 8\\\") {\\n393\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Should include line number\\\")\\n394\\t\\t}\\n395\\t\\tif !contains(errorMsg, \\\"field server.port\\\") {\\n396\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Should include field path\\\")\\n397\\t\\t}\\n398\\t\\tif !contains(errorMsg, \\\"expected integer\\\") {\\n399\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Should include expected type\\\")\\n400\\t\\t}\\n401\\t\\tif !contains(errorMsg, \\\"got string\\\") {\\n402\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Should include actual type\\\")\\n403\\t\\t}\\n404\\t\\n405\\t\\t// Verify Context() method\\n406\\t\\tctxMsg := err.Context()\\n407\\t\\texpectedCtx := \\\"field: server.port, expected type: integer, actual type: string, got integer\\n418\\t// - Expected boolean, got string\\n419\\t// - Expected array, got scalar\\n420\\t// - Expected object, got null\\n421\\tfunc TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes(t *testing.T) {\\n422\\t\\ttestCases := []struct {\\n423\\t\\t\\tname         string\\n424\\t\\t\\tfieldPath    string\\n425\\t\\t\\texpectedType string\\n426\\t\\t\\tactualType   string\\n427\\t\\t\\tvalue got\\\",\\n452\\t\\t\\t\\tfieldPath:    \\\"hosts\\\",\\n453\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedType: \\\"array\\\",\\n454\\t\\t\\t\\tactualType:   \\\"string\\\",\\n455\\t\\t\\t\\tvalue:        \\\"localhost\\\",\\n456\\t\\t\\t},\\n457\\t\\t\\t{\\n458\\t\\t\\t\\tname:         \\\"object expected, null got\\\",\\n459\\t\\t\\t\\tfieldPath:    \\\"config\\\",\\n460\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedType: \\\"object\\\",\\n461\\t\\t\\t\\tactualType:   \\\"null\\\",\\n462\\t\\t\\t\\tvalue:        \\\"null\\\",\\n463\\t\\t\\t},\\n464\\t\\t}\\n465\\t\\n466\\t\\tfor _, tt := range testCases {\\n467\\t\\t\\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\\n468\\t\\t\\t\\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\\n469\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFilePath:     \\\"config.yaml\\\",\\n470\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFieldPath: ============================================================================\\n718\\t\\n719\\t// TestErrorFormatConsistency verifies that error formats follow consistent patterns\\n720\\t//\\n721\\t// Consistency rules:\\n722\\t// 1. File path always present if available\\n723\\t// 2. Position (line, column) follows file path with \\\"at line <line>, column <column>\\\" format\\n724\\t// 3. Field paths use \\\"at field <path>\\\" or \\\"field <path>:\\\" format\\n725\\t// 4. Type mismatches use \\\"expected <expected>, got <actual>\\\" format\\n726\\t// 5. Constraints use \\\"(constraint: <details>)\\\" or \\\"constraint: <details>\\\" format\\n727\\tfunc TestErrorFormatConsistency(t *testing.T) {\\n728\\t\\t// Test ParseError format {\\n734\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"ParseError should include position with 'at line X, column Y'\\\")\\n735\\t\\t}\\n736\\t\\n737\\t\\t// Test ValidationError format consistency\\n738\\t\\tvalidationErr := &TypeMismatchError{\\n748\\t\\t\\tFilePath:     \\\"config.yaml\\\",\\n749\\t\\t\\tFieldPath:    \\\"server.port\\\",\\n750\\t\\t\\tExpectedType: \\\"integer\\\",\\n751\\t\\t\\tActualType: recognized\\n825\\tfunc TestErrorRecognition(t *testing.T) {\\n826\\t\\t// Test ParseError recognition\\n827\\t\\tparseErr := NewParseError(\\\"test.yaml\\\", \\\"test\\\", 1, 1, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"GetYAMLErrorType() = %v, expected %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n\nfunc TestWrapError(t *testing.T) {\n\tinnerErr := errors.New(\"inner error\")\n\twrapped := fmt.Errorf(\"context message: %w\", innerErr)\n\n\tif wrapped == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"wrapped error returned nil\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that the error message contains the context\n\tmsg := wrapped.Error()\n\tif !contains(msg, \"context message\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrapped error message should contain context, got: %s\", msg)\n\t}\n\tif !contains(msg, \"inner error\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrapped error message should contain inner error, got: %s\", msg)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that unwrapping returns the original error\n\tif unwrapped := errors.Unwrap(wrapped); unwrapped != innerErr {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"unwrapped error should be the original error, got: %v\", unwrapped)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestIsParseError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\terr      error\n\t\texpected bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nil error returns false\",\n\t\t\terr:      nil,\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"standard error returns false\",\n\t\t\terr:      errors.New(\"standard error\"),\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"ParseError returns true\",\n\t\t\terr:      &ParseError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"},\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"wrapped ParseError returns true\",\n\t\t\terr:      fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", &ParseError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"}),\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"ValidationError returns false\",\n\t\t\terr:      &ValidationError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"},\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := IsParseError(tt.err)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"IsParseError() = %v, expected %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestNewParseError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\tfilePath     string\n\t\tmessage %q, want %q\", err.Message, tt.message)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err.Line != tt.line {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Line = %d, want %d\", err.Line, tt.line)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err.Column != tt.column tt.wantErrorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify Error() method returns formatted message with position\n\t\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n\t\t\tif errorMsg != tt.wantMessage {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", errorMsg, tt.wantMessage)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify it's recognized as a YAMLError\n\t\t\tif !IsYAMLError(err) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"NewParseError() should implement YAMLError interface\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify it's recognized as a ParseError\n\t\t\tif !IsParseError(err) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"NewParseError() should be recognized as ParseError\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Helper function to check if a string contains a substring\nfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || len(s) > len(substr) && containsHelper(s, substr))\n}\n\nfunc containsHelper(s, substr string) bool {\n\tfor i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {\n\t\tif s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc TestNewValidationError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\tfilePath     string\n\t\tmessage      string\n\t\tfieldPath type\")\n317\t\t}\n318\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"validation failed\") {\n319\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include message\")\n320\t\t}\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t// TestValidationErrorComplete demonstrates complete ValidationError with all components\n324\t//\n325\t// Format breakdown:\n326\t// - Location: \"<file>:<line>:<column>\" or \"<file>\"\n327\t// - Error type: \"validation error in\"\n328\t// - Field path: \"at field <field-path>\"\n329\t// - Message: The specific validation failure\n330\t// - Constraint: \"(constraint: <details>)\"\n331\t//\n332\t// Example:\n333\t//\n334\t//\tvalidation error in deployment.yaml at line 22, column 18 at field spec.template.spec.containers[0].image: invalid image tag (constraint: must match registry/*:tag pattern)\n335\tfunc TestValidationErrorComplete(t *testing.T) {\n336\t\terr := NewValidationError(\"deployment.yaml\", \"invalid image tag\", \"spec.template.spec.containers[0].image\", \"must match registry/*:tag pattern\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 22, 18, ErrorTypeValidation, \"\")\n337\t\n338\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n339\t\n340\t\t// Verify all components\n341\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"deployment.yaml\") {\n342\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n343\t\t}\n344\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 22, column 18\") {\n345\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line and column\")\n346\t\t}\n347\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at field spec.template.spec.containers[0].image\") {\n348\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field path\")\n349\t\t}\n350\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"invalid image tag\") {\n351\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include message\")\n352\t\t}\n353\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"constraint: must match registry/*:tag pattern\") {\n354\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include constraint\")\n355\t\t}\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t// ============================================================================\n359\t// Section 3: Type mismatch errors\n360\t// ============================================================================\n361\t\n362\t// TestTypeMismatchErrorFormat demonstrates type mismatch error format\n363\t//\n364\t// Format: \"type mismatch in <file> at line <line>, field <field-path>: expected <expected>, got <actual>\"\n365\t//\n366\t// Example output:\n367\t//\n368\t//\ttype mismatch in config.yaml at line 8, field server.port: expected integer, got string\n369\tfunc TestTypeMismatchErrorFormat(t *testing.T) {\n370\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n371\t\t\tFilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n372\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"server.port\",\n373\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n374\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n375\t\t\tValue:        \"8080\",\n376\t\t\tLine:         8,\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\texpected := \"type mismatch in config.yaml at line 8, field server.port: expected integer, got string\"\n380\t\tif err.Error() != expected {\n381\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), expected)\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\t// Verify format\n385\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n386\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"type mismatch in\") {\n387\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include error type\")\n388\t\t}\n389\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"config.yaml\") {\n390\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n391\t\t}\n392\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 8\") {\n393\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n394\t\t}\n395\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"field server.port\") {\n396\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field path\")\n397\t\t}\n398\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected integer\") {\n399\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include expected type\")\n400\t\t}\n401\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"got string\") {\n402\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include actual type\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t// Verify Context() method\n406\t\tctxMsg := err.Context()\n407\t\texpectedCtx := \"field: server.port, expected type: integer, actual type: string, value: 8080\"\n408\t\tif ctxMsg != expectedCtx {\n409\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Context() = %q, want %q\", ctxMsg, expectedCtx)\n410\t\t}\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\t// TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes documents type mismatch errors for various type combinations\n414\t//\n415\t// Common type mismatches:\n416\t// - Expected integer, got string\n417\t// - Expected string, got integer\n418\t// - Expected boolean, got string\n419\t// - Expected array, got scalar\n420\t// - Expected object, got null\n421\tfunc TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes(t *testing.T) {\n422\t\ttestCases := []struct {\n423\t\t\tname         string\n424\t\t\tfieldPath    string\n425\t\t\texpectedType string\n426\t\t\tactualType   string\n427\t\t\tvalue        string\n428\t\t}{\n429\t\t\t{\n430\t\t\t\tname:         \"integer expected, string got\",\n431\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"port\",\n432\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"integer\",\n433\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n434\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"8080\",\n435\t\t\t},\n436\t\t\t{\n437\t\t\t\tname:         \"integer expected, boolean got\",\n438\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"timeout\",\n439\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"integer\",\n440\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"boolean\",\n441\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"true\",\n442\t\t\t},\n443\t\t\t{\n444\t\t\t\tname:         \"boolean expected, string got\",\n445\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"enabled\",\n446\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"boolean\",\n447\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n448\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"yes\",\n449\t\t\t},\n450\t\t\t{\n451\t\t\t\tname:         \"array expected, string got\",\n452\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"hosts\",\n453\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"array\",\n454\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"string\",\n455\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"localhost\",\n456\t\t\t},\n457\t\t\t{\n458\t\t\t\tname:         \"object expected, null got\",\n459\t\t\t\tfieldPath:    \"config\",\n460\t\t\t\texpectedType: \"object\",\n461\t\t\t\tactualType:   \"null\",\n462\t\t\t\tvalue:        \"null\",\n463\t\t\t},\n464\t\t}\n465\t\n466\t\tfor _, tt := range testCases {\n467\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n468\t\t\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n469\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n470\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    tt.fieldPath,\n471\t\t\t\t\tExpectedType: tt.expectedType,\n472\t\t\t\t\tActualType:   tt.actualType,\n473\t\t\t\t\tValue:        tt.value,\n474\t\t\t\t\tLine:         10,\n475\t\t\t\t}\n476\t\n477\t\t\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n478\t\n479\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.fieldPath) {\n480\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include field path '%s'\", tt.fieldPath)\n481\t\t\t\t}\n482\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.expectedType) {\n483\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include expected type '%s'\", tt.expectedType)\n484\t\t\t\t}\n485\t\t\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, tt.actualType) {\n486\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Should include actual type '%s'\", tt.actualType)\n487\t\t\t\t}\n488\t\n489\t\t\t\t// Verify error type\n490\t\t\t\tif err.YAMLErrorType() != ErrorTypeTypeMismatch {\n491\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"YAMLErrorType() = %q, want %q\", err.YAMLErrorType(), ErrorTypeTypeMismatch)\n492\t\t\t\t}\n493\t\n494\t\t\t\t// Verify error code\n495\t\t\t\tif err.Code() != ErrCodeTypeMismatch {\n496\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Code() = %q, want %q\", err.Code(), ErrCodeTypeMismatch)\n497\t\t\t\t}\n498\t\t\t})\n499\t\t}\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// TestTypeMismatchNestedFields demonstrates type mismatch in nested field paths\n503\t//\n504\t// Example:\n505\t//\n506\t//\ttype mismatch in app.yaml at line 35, field servers.api.responses[0].statusCode: expected integer, got string\n507\tfunc TestTypeMismatchNestedFields(t *testing.T) {\n508\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n509\t\t\tFilePath:     \"app.yaml\",\n510\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"servers.api.responses[0].statusCode\",\n511\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n512\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n513\t\t\tValue:        \"\\\"200\\\"\",\n514\t\t\tLine:         35,\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n518\t\n519\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"app.yaml\") {\n520\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n521\t\t}\n522\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 35\") {\n523\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n524\t\t}\n525\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"field servers.api.responses[0].statusCode\") {\n526\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include nested field path\")\n527\t\t}\n528\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected integer, got string\") {\n529\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include type information\")\n530\t\t}\n531\t\n532\t\t// Verify context\n533\t\tctxMsg := err.Context()\n534\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"field: servers.api.responses[0].statusCode\") {\n535\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include field path\")\n536\t\t}\n537\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"expected type: integer\") {\n538\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include expected type\")\n539\t\t}\n540\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"actual type: string\") {\n541\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include actual type\")\n542\t\t}\n543\t}\n544\t\n545\t// TestTypeMismatchWithoutLine demonstrates type mismatch error without line number\n546\t//\n547\t// Format: \"type mismatch in <file>, field <field-path>: expected <expected>, got <actual>\"\n548\t//\n549\t// Example output:\n550\t//\n551\t//\ttype mismatch in data.yaml, field database.timeout: expected integer, got boolean\n552\tfunc TestTypeMismatchWithoutLine(t *testing.T) {\n553\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n554\t\t\tFilePath:     \"data.yaml\",\n555\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"database.timeout\",\n556\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n557\t\t\tActualType:   \"boolean\",\n558\t\t\tValue:        \"true\",\n559\t\t\tLine:         0,\n560\t\t}\n561\t\n562\t\texpected := \"type mismatch in data.yaml, field database.timeout: expected integer, got boolean\"\n563\t\tif err.Error() != expected {\n564\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), expected)\n565\t\t}\n566\t\n567\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n568\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"type mismatch in data.yaml\") {\n569\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path without line number\")\n570\t\t}\n571\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"field database.timeout\") {\n572\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field path\")\n573\t\t}\n574\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected integer, got boolean\") {\n575\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include type information\")\n576\t\t}\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// ============================================================================\n580\t// Section 4: Real-world error scenarios\n581\t// ============================================================================\n582\t\n583\t// TestRealWorldConfigFileError demonstrates a realistic configuration file error scenario\n584\t//\n585\t// Scenario: Invalid port configuration in service config\n586\t//\n587\t// config.yaml:\n588\t//\n589\t//\tservices:\n590\t//\t  - name: web\n591\t//\t    port: \"8080\"  # ERROR: should be integer, not string\n592\t//\n593\t// Error output:\n594\t//\n595\t//\ttype mismatch in config.yaml at line 5, field services[0].port: expected integer, got string\n596\tfunc TestRealWorldConfigFileError(t *testing.T) {\n597\t\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n598\t\t\tFilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n599\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"services[0].port\",\n600\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n601\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n602\t\t\tValue:        \"\\\"8080\\\"\",\n603\t\t\tLine:         5,\n604\t\t}\n605\t\n606\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n607\t\n608\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"config.yaml\") {\n609\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n610\t\t}\n611\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 5\") {\n612\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n613\t\t}\n614\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"field services[0].port\") {\n615\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field with array index\")\n616\t\t}\n617\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected integer, got string\") {\n618\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include type mismatch\")\n619\t\t}\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// TestRealWorldValidationError demonstrates a realistic validation error scenario\n623\t//\n624\t// Scenario: Port number out of valid range\n625\t//\n626\t// config.yaml:\n627\t//\n628\t//\tserver:\n629\t//\t  port: 70000  # ERROR: out of range (1-65535)\n630\t//\n631\t// Error output:\n632\t//\n633\t//\tconstraint violation in config.yaml at line 10, field server.port: must be between 1-65535\n634\tfunc TestRealWorldValidationError(t *testing.T) {\n635\t\terr := &ConstraintError{\n636\t\t\tFilePath:       \"config.yaml\",\n637\t\t\tFieldPath:      \"server.port\",\n638\t\t\tConstraintType: \"range\",\n639\t\t\tConstraint:     \"must be between 1-65535\",\n640\t\t\tValue:          \"70000\",\n641\t\t\tLine:           10,\n642\t\t}\n643\t\n644\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n645\t\n646\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"config.yaml\") {\n647\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n648\t\t}\n649\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 10\") {\n650\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n651\t\t}\n652\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"field server.port\") {\n653\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field path\")\n654\t\t}\n655\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"must be between 1-65535\") {\n656\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include constraint\")\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Verify context\n660\t\tctxMsg := err.Context()\n661\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"field: server.port\") {\n662\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include field path\")\n663\t\t}\n664\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"constraint: must be between 1-65535\") {\n665\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include constraint\")\n666\t\t}\n667\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"value: 70000\") {\n668\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include actual value\")\n669\t\t}\n670\t}\n671\t\n672\t// TestRealWorldFieldNotFoundError demonstrates a realistic missing field error scenario\n673\t//\n674\t// Scenario: Required field is missing\n675\t//\n676\t// config.yaml:\n677\t//\n678\t//\tdatabase:\n679\t//\t  host: localhost\n680\t//\t  # ERROR: missing required field 'port'\n681\t//\n682\t// Error output:\n683\t//\n684\t//\trequired field missing in config.yaml at line 8: database.port\n685\tfunc TestRealWorldFieldNotFoundError(t *testing.T) {\n686\t\terr := NewFieldNotFoundError(\"config.yaml\", \"database.port\", 8, \"\")\n687\t\n688\t\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n689\t\n690\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"required field missing\") {\n691\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include error type\")\n692\t\t}\n693\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"config.yaml\") {\n694\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n695\t\t}\n696\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 8\") {\n697\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n698\t\t}\n699\t\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"database.port\") {\n700\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should include field path\")\n701\t\t}\n702\t\n703\t\t// Verify context\n704\t\tctxMsg := err.Context()\n705\t\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"required field not found: database.port\") {\n706\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include field path\")\n707\t\t}\n708\t\n709\t\t// Verify error type\n710\t\tif err.YAMLErrorType() != ErrorTypeFieldNotFound {\n711\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"YAMLErrorType() = %q, want %q\", err.YAMLErrorType(), ErrorTypeFieldNotFound)\n712\t\t}\n713\t}\n714\t\n715\t// ============================================================================\n716\t// Section 5: Error message format consistency\n717\t// ============================================================================\n718\t\n719\t// TestErrorFormatConsistency verifies that error formats follow consistent patterns\n720\t//\n721\t// Consistency rules:\n722\t// 1. File path always present if available\n723\t// 2. Position (line, column) follows file path with \"at line <line>, column <column>\" format\n724\t// 3. Field paths use \"at field <path>\" or \"field <path>:\" format\n725\t// 4. Type mismatches use \"expected <expected>, got <actual>\" format\n726\t// 5. Constraints use \"(constraint: <details>)\" or \"constraint: <details>\" format\n727\tfunc TestErrorFormatConsistency(t *testing.T) {\n728\t\t// Test ParseError format consistency\n729\t\tparseErr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"test\", 10, 5, \"\", \"\", \"\")\n730\t\tif !contains(parseErr.Error(), \"parse error in\") {\n731\t\t\tt.Error(\"ParseError should start with 'parse error in'\")\n732\t\t}\n733\t\tif !contains(parseErr.Error(), \"at line 10, column 5\") {\n734\t\t\tt.Error(\"ParseError should include position with 'at line X, column Y'\")\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\t// Test ValidationError format consistency\n738\t\tvalidationErr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"test\", \"server.port\", \"constraint\", \"\", 10, 5, \"\", \"\")\n739\t\tif !contains(validationErr.Error(), \"validation error in\") {\n740\t\t\tt.Error(\"ValidationError should start with 'validation error in'\")\n741\t\t}\n742\t\tif !contains(validationErr.Error(), \"at field server.port\") {\n743\t\t\tt.Error(\"ValidationError should include 'at field <path>'\")\n744\t\t}\n745\t\n746\t\t// Test TypeMismatchError format consistency\n747\t\ttypeErr := &TypeMismatchError{\n748\t\t\tFilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n749\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"server.port\",\n750\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n751\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n752\t\t\tLine:         10,\n753\t\t}\n754\t\tif !contains(typeErr.Error(), \"type mismatch in\") {\n755\t\t\tt.Error(\"TypeMismatchError should start with 'type mismatch in'\")\n756\t\t}\n757\t\tif !contains(typeErr.Error(), \"expected integer, got string\") {\n758\t\t\tt.Error(\"TypeMismatchError should use 'expected X, got Y' format\")\n759\t\t}\n760\t\n761\t\t// Test ConstraintError format consistency\n762\t\tconstraintErr := &ConstraintError{\n763\t\t\tFilePath:       \"config.yaml\",\n764\t\t\tFieldPath:      \"server.port\",\n765\t\t\tConstraint:     \"must be between 1-65535\",\n766\t\t\tLine:           10,\n767\t\t}\n768\t\tif !contains(constraintErr.Error(), \"constraint violation in\") {\n769\t\t\tt.Error(\"ConstraintError should start with 'constraint violation in'\")\n770\t\t}\n771\t\tif !contains(constraintErr.Error(), \"must be between 1-65535\") {\n772\t\t\tt.Error(\"ConstraintError should include constraint\")\n773\t\t}\n774\t}\n775\t\n776\t// TestErrorContextFormat verifies that Context() methods provide structured information\n777\tfunc TestErrorContextFormat(t *testing.T) {\n778\t\t// Test TypeMismatchError Context()\n779\t\ttypeErr := &TypeMismatchError{\n780\t\t\tFieldPath:    \"server.port\",\n781\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n782\t\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n783\t\t\tValue:        \"8080\",\n784\t\t}\n785\t\tctx := typeErr.Context()\n786\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"field: server.port\") {\n787\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'field: <path>'\")\n788\t\t}\n789\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"expected type: integer\") {\n790\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'expected type: <type>'\")\n791\t\t}\n792\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"actual type: string\") {\n793\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'actual type: <type>'\")\n794\t\t}\n795\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"value: 8080\") {\n796\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'value: <value>'\")\n797\t\t}\n798\t\n799\t\t// Test ConstraintError Context()\n800\t\tconstraintErr := &ConstraintError{\n801\t\t\tFieldPath:  \"server.port\",\n802\t\t\tConstraint: \"must be between 1-65535\",\n803\t\t\tValue:      \"70000\",\n804\t\t}\n805\t\tctx = constraintErr.Context()\n806\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"field: server.port\") {\n807\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'field: <path>'\")\n808\t\t}\n809\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"constraint: must be between 1-65535\") {\n810\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'constraint: <constraint>'\")\n811\t\t}\n812\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"value: 70000\") {\n813\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'value: <value>'\")\n814\t\t}\n815\t\n816\t\t// Test FieldNotFoundError Context()\n817\t\tfieldErr := NewFieldNotFoundError(\"config.yaml\", \"database.host\", 5, \"\")\n818\t\tctx = fieldErr.Context()\n819\t\tif !contains(ctx, \"required field not found: database.host\") {\n820\t\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include 'required field not found: <path>'\")\n821\t\t}\n822\t}\n823\t\n824\t// TestErrorRecognition verifies that error types are correctly recognized\n825\tfunc TestErrorRecognition(t *testing.T) {\n826\t\t// Test ParseError recognition\n827\t\tparseErr := NewParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"test\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\", \"\")\n828\t\tif !IsParseError(parseErr) {\n829\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should recognize ParseError\")\n830\t\t}\n831\t\tif !IsYAMLError(parseErr) {\n832\t\t\tt.Error(\"ParseError should be a YAMLError\")\n833\t\t}\n834\t\n835\t\t// Test ValidationError recognition\n836\t\tvalidationErr := NewValidationError(\"test.yaml\", \"test\", \"field\", \"constraint\", \"\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\")\n837\t\tif !IsValidationError(validationErr) {\n838\t\t\tt.Error(\"Should recognize ValidationError\")\n839\t\t}\n840\t\tif !IsYAMLError(validationErr) {\n841\t\t\tt.Error(\"ValidationError should be a YAMLError\")\n842\t\t}\n843\t\n844\t\t// Test TypeMismatchError recognition\n845\t\ttypeErr := &TypeMismatchError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"}\n846\t\tif !IsYAMLError(typeErr) {\n847\t\t\tt.Error(\"TypeMismatchError should be a YAMLError\")\n848\t\t}\n849\t\tif Package yamlutil test file documenting error message formats\n//\n// This test file demonstrates and verifies all error message formats across the ARMOR codebase.\n// It serves as both test coverage and documentation for how errors are formatted and displayed.\n//\n// Error Message Format Categories\n//\n// 1. ParseError with line:column\n//    - Format: \"parse error in <file> at line <line>, column <column>: <message>\"\n//    - Example: \"parse error in config.yaml at line 10, column 5: missing colon\"\n//\n// 2. ValidationError with field paths\n//    - Format: \"validation error in <file> at line <line>, column <column> at field <field-path>: <message> (constraint: <constraint>)\"\n//    - Example: \"validation error in config.yaml at line 15, column 12 at field server.port: port must be between 1 and 65535 (constraint: must be between 1-65535)\"\n//\n// 3. Type mismatch errors\n//    - Format: \"type mismatch in <file> at line <line>, field <field-path>: expected <expected>, got <actual>\"\n//    - Example: \"type mismatch in config.yaml at line 8, field server.port: expected integer, got string\"\n\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n)\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Section 1: ParseError with line:column\n// ============================================================================\n\n// TestParseErrorLineColumnFullFormat demonstrates complete ParseError format with all location components\n//\n// Format: \"parse error in <file> at line <line>, column <column>: <message>\"\n//\n// Example output:\n//\n//\tparse error in config.yaml at line 10, column 5: missing colon\nfunc TestParseErrorLineColumnFullFormat(t *testing.T) {\n\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"missing colon\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"\", \"\")\n\n\texpected := \"parse error in config.yaml at line 10, column 5: missing colon\"\n\tif err.Error() != expected {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), expected)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify format components\n\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"config.yaml\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file path\")\n\t}\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"at line 10\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include line number\")\n\t}\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"column 5\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include column number\")\n\t}\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"missing colon\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include error message\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestParseErrorLineColumnVariations documents different location formatting scenarios\nfunc TestParseErrorLineColumnVariations(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tfilePath string\n\t\tmessage  string\n\t\tline     int\n\t\tcolumn   int\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"file + line + column (most specific)\",\n\t\t\tfilePath: \"config.yaml\",\n\t\t\tmessage:  \"test\",\n\t\t\tline:     10,\n\t\t\tcolumn:   5,\n\t\t\texpected: \"parse error in config.yaml at line 10, column 5: test\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"file + line only\",\n\t\t\tfilePath: \"config.yaml\",\n\t\t\tmessage:  \"test\",\n\t\t\tline:     10,\n\t\t\tcolumn:   0,\n\t\t\texpected: \"parse error in config.yaml at line 10: test\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"file only\",\n\t\t\tfilePath: \"config.yaml\",\n\t\t\tmessage:  \"test\",\n\t\t\tline:     0,\n\t\t\tcolumn:   0,\n\t\t\texpected: \"parse error in config.yaml: test\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := NewParseError(tt.filePath, tt.message, tt.line, tt.column, \"\", \"\", \"\")\n\t\t\tif err.Error() != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestParseErrorWithExpectedActual demonstrates ParseError with type information\n//\n// Format: \"parse error in <file> at line <line>, column <column>: <message> (expected: <expected>, actual: <actual>)\"\n//\n// Example output:\n//\n//\tparse error in schema.yaml at line 7, column 12: type mismatch (expected: string, actual: integer)\nfunc TestParseErrorWithExpectedActual(t *testing.T) {\n\terr := NewParseError(\"schema.yaml\", \"type err.Error() != expected {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), expected)\n\t}\n\n\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected: string\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should ErrCodeConstraintViolation,\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := NewParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"test message\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationErrorComplete demonstrates complete ValidationError with all components\n//\n// Format breakdown:\n// - Location: \"<file>:<line>:<column>\" or \"<file>\"\n// - expectedCtx)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes documents type mismatch errors for various type combinations\n//\n// Common type mismatches:\n// - Expected integer, got string\n// - Expected string, got integer\n// - Expected boolean, got string\n// - Expected array, got scalar\n// - Expected object, got null\nfunc TestTypeMismatchVariousTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestCases := []struct err.YAMLErrorType() != ErrorTypeTypeMismatch {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"YAMLErrorType() = %q, want %q\", err.YAMLErrorType(), ErrorTypeTypeMismatch)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify error code\n\t\t\tif err.Code() != ErrCodeTypeMismatch {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Code() = %q, want %q\", err.Code(), ErrCodeTypeMismatch)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestTypeMismatchNestedFields demonstrates type mismatch *testing.T) {\n\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     \"app.yaml\",\n\t\tFieldPath:    \"servers.api.responses[0].statusCode\",\n\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\tActualType:   \"string\",\n\t\tValue:        \"\\\"200\\\"\",\n\t\tLine:         35,\n\t}\n\n\terrorMsg := err.Error()\n\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"app.yaml\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include file {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include nested field path\")\n\t}\n\tif !contains(errorMsg, \"expected integer, got string\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Should include type information\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify type: integer\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include expected type\")\n\t}\n\tif !contains(ctxMsg, \"actual type: string\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Context should include actual type\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// output:\n//\n//\ttype mismatch in data.yaml, field database.timeout: expected integer, got boolean\nfunc TestTypeMismatchWithoutLine(t *testing.T) {\n\terr := &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     \"data.yaml\",\n\t\tFieldPath:    \"database.timeout\",\n\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\tActualType:   \"boolean\",\n\t\tValue:        \"true\",\n\t\tLine:         0,\n\t}\n\n\texpected := \"type mismatch in data.yaml, field database.timeout: expected integer, got boolean\"\n\tif err.Error() != expected {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error() = %q, want %q\", err.Error(), expected)\n\t}\n\n\terrorMsg :=
